label: ablation_boot_full
study: Boot-time ablation for the battery MCP server. Each section measures one layer of the boot pipeline; comparisons within a section are paired (same process, same cells, warmed TF kernels).
hardware_note: Apple Silicon CPU, no GPU. Numbers will differ on x86 / GPU.

data_load.what: Fetch + Python preprocess for all cells, no TF predict.
data_load.methodology: Monkey-patch CouchDBClient.fetch_cycles to record per-call wall_ms; remainder of preprocess_cell_from_couchdb is attributed to scipy preprocess.

precompute_strategy.what: How to populate _CACHE from preprocessed tensors at boot.
precompute_strategy.methodology: Same N cells, in-memory only (disk cache wiped each round, _CACHE cleared between runs).
precompute_strategy.verdict: Roughly a wash on Apple Silicon CPU at N=10. Collapsing 4N→4 predict() calls trades dispatch savings for larger kernels that the CPU scheduler doesn't parallelize as well. Worth keeping for GPU / much larger N.

in_memory_cache.what: Cost of one tool-call lookup after _boot() populates _CACHE.
in_memory_cache.methodology: Mean over 1000 sequential dict accesses to _CACHE[cell]['rul_trajectory'].
in_memory_cache.verdict: Dominant intra-process win. Limitation: dict dies when MCP subprocess exits — every fresh tool call still pays boot cost. Addressed by the disk_cache layer below.

disk_cache.what: Disk-backed .npz cache that survives MCP subprocess restarts.
disk_cache.methodology: Same boot sequence twice. Round 1: wipe artifacts/cache/, run precompute_cells_fully_batched (writes .npz per cell). Round 2: clear in-memory _CACHE only, run precompute_cells_fully_batched again — every cell short-circuits via _try_load_from_disk.

before_optimization.name: cold boot (no disk cache, must compute)

after_optimization.name: warm boot (loads .npz from disk, skips TF predict)

disk_cache.verdict: Persists across MCP subprocess restarts. Makes every cold MCP call after the first invocation skip the boot precompute.
